title: Visualise funnel charts for sales or marketing - introducing Funnels for monday.com

posts.author: BraveCarto (Tomasz Kustrzynski)
posts.cookedHtml:
Funnels for sales and marketing

Omnitas.dev presents Funnels - an app which lets you draw beautiful charts for sales/marketing funnels and analyse journey of items through the workflow.

Omnitas.dev is part of Omnitas Consulting - monday.com’s Best Professional Service Partner.

Funnels won the main prize in monday.com Apps Marketplace Challenge (you can see the entry on devpost.com) and we’re proud to be able to bring it to you via Apps Marketplace now!

Funnels for monday.com on the Marketplace

Functionality

The app adds 2 Board Views:

Funnel Board View

This view lets you visualise traditional sales or marketing funnel showing number of items per status. Graph assumes that reaching certain state means an items went through all previous states.

funnel-view-dark2614×982 215 KB

Tree / Journey Board View

This view shows transitions of items between statuses and takes into account the whole activity log of items.

tree-view-dark2412×1394 315 KB

Documentation and more info

  • Live demos for Funnel view and Tree/Journey view.
  • Watch the Youtube video.
  • Documentation on omnitas.dev

Pricing

14 day trial followed by subscription of $19/month or $209/year.

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are you interested in understanding the full power and capabilities of Funnels monday.com app?
Register for our future webinar waitlist and we will send you an invite!

In the meantime we would love to hear all your feedback!

posts.author: Gabo (Gabriel Braun)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looks amazing, my only recomendation is that you also have a vertical style sales funnel as many people are used to that view.

We are also a monday partner (in Mexico and Central America) and it will be amazing if we could get a referral fee if we bring your app into the accounts that we we serve.

A

posts.author: BraveCarto (Tomasz Kustrzynski)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s a great suggestion @Gabo. We’re working on this as well as a dashboard view, which would also use vertical funnel style.

I’ll send you a DM so that we can talk about cooperation.
Cheers!
